metadata.name: formal_learning_theory_proof_world_model
metadata.description: Typed graph encoding proof methods, theorems, infrastructure, mathematical domains, and their relationships. Consumable by agents for proof search routing and by humans for proof architecture navigation.
metadata.provenance_note: All theorem nodes are extracted from the kernel source files via grep. Line numbers, file paths, and visibility are from the kernel codebase. Method annotations on curated theorems are hand-assigned based on proof inspection. Edges without direct kernel provenance are marked with provenance: discovery_repo for future cross-referencing.

separations.label: pac_not_implies_online
separations.exploits: monotonicity bounds shattering but not adversarial depth

separations.label: analytic_nonborel_set_gives_measTarget_separation
separations.witness: singleton class over analytic non-Borel A

complete_theorem_inventory.description: All 278 theorem/lemma declarations in the kernel, grouped by file. Each entry has id (name), line number, kind (theorem/lemma), and visibility (private/public). This is the complete inventory; the curated theorem nodes above are representative selections with method and domain annotations.

paradigm_locking.reason: requires enumeration structure of text presentations
requires pointwise convergence on prefix streams
connects descriptive set theory to learning theory; required infrastructure not in original premise
countable selection via Nat.find; applies to any countable enumeration

method_compositions.label: Sauer-Shelah feeds symmetrization (growth function bound → UC)
product factorization feeds finite Jensen (MGF → soft-max)
adversary strategy uses tree induction (game search)
potential function guides adversary (version space Ldim comparison)

method_incompatibilities.reason: symmetrization requires infinite sample space; on finite X, direct enumeration suffices
concentration requires MeasureTheory.Measure in goal type
adversary construction requires sequential state (OnlineLearner signature)

produced_by_extension.mechanism: making WellBehavedVC a typeclass raised the question: is it equivalent to KrappWirth?
formalizing joint learner measurability as a gate raised the question: which non-neural architectures pass?
separation proved the gap exists; interpolation asked what operation produces it
interpolation well-behavedness implies the descent: Borel level can fail for composed classes

routing_rules.description: Given a GoalProfile, these rules predict which methods and infrastructure are relevant.

rules.condition: goal mentions OnlineLearner or State or predict
goal mentions List or DataStream or prefix or Countable
goal mentions AnalyticSet or NullMeasurableSet or BorelSpace
goal mentions FinitePMF or klDiv or posterior
goal mentions Infinite X and VCDim
rules.reason: produces 2^{2m} not GrowthFunction; route to M8
